title: ‘Things are going to get worse’: Chris Bowen under fire over Australia’s fuel shortage
description: Shadow Energy Minister Dan Tehan says Energy Minister Chris Bowen is under fire over Australia’s fuel shortage.   “We have been asking him, and we actually wrote him a letter on the 10th of March saying, ‘Where are the fuel shortages, and what are you doing to address them?’” Mr Tehan told Sky News host Chris Kenny.  “This is the first time today he’s actually been able to admit that there’s a problem.”

title: Dan Tehan must build a ‘compelling case’ for the people amid rise in One Nation support
description: Shadow Energy Minister Dan Tehan discusses how he’s actively working to develop “clear-cut” policies amid a rise in support for One Nation.    “The thing that I'm concentrating on is Farrer … we have to put a compelling case to the voters in Farrer,” Mr Tehan told Sky News host Chris Kenny.  “We’ve got to have very clear-cut policies that we can take to the people of Farrer.”

title: Anthony Albanese has failed Australians on social cohesion: Dan Tehan
description: Shadow Energy Minister Dan Tehan details how Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has failed on social cohesion in Australia.   “It sends a terrible message about social cohesion, it shows that the prime minister has presided basically on the breakdown of social cohesion in this country,” Mr Tehan told Sky News host Chris Kenny.  “We are seeing the disintegration of social cohesion in this nation.”

title: One Nation ‘fighting’ for the rights of Australians: Pauline Hanson
description: One Nation Leader Pauline Hanson says the One Nation Party is out there “fighting” for the rights of Australians.  Ms Hanson told Sky News host Andrew Bolt that she was “the first one” to come out against the Voice to Parliament.  “It goes on and on, what we have been able to achieve in this parliament for the people.”

title: ‘Gritty stalemate’: Expert warns Middle East conflict could last several months
description: IPA’s Adjunct Fellow Peter Jennings discusses the longevity of the Middle East conflict.   “It’s come down to a pretty gritty stalemate,” Mr Jennings told Sky News host Paul Murray.   “I think the Americans would have liked to have made more progress by now in destroying the capacity.  “The Iranian leadership, if they can just hang out for a few weeks longer, political factors will drive the Americans to conclude that they can’t go on.  “How long does that mean the war will go on? I think we are talking about several months.”

title: Trump says ‘productive conversations’ between US and Iran, DOW postpones military strikes
description: Sky News host James Macpherson says “productive conversations” have occurred between the US and Iran as the Department of War has been instructed to postpone strikes against Iranian power plants and energy infrastructure.

title: ‘Denial and delusion’: Albanese ‘downplays’ Lakemba mosque confrontation
description: Sky News host Chris Kenny claims Prime Minister Anthony Albanese was “rattled” by his visit to the Lakemba mosque.   “Anthony Albanese is so coy about the threat of Islamist extremism that he never utters the words,” Mr Kenny said.  “Listen to the Prime Minister downplay it. This is denial and delusion.  “The more he averts his eyes to this problem, the more he indulges in jihad denial, the more this country will be endangered, and we will all suffer.”

title: ‘Times are tough’: ABC staff to stage 24-hour strike after pay offer rejected
description: Sky News host Caleb Bond discusses the ABC going on a 24-hour strike following a pay offer rejection.   “Times are tough, it’s tough in business,” Mr Bond said.   “They are reasonably well paid comparatively at the ABC, compared to the rest of journalism.”

title: Major flood alerts across NT as ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle pushes west
description: Sky News Weather Senior Meteorologist Rob Sharpe analyses flooding spreading across the Top End as ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle moves west.  “Of course, we’ve still got those flood warnings and watches in place for the territory,” Mr Sharpe said.   “Major flood warning for the Waterhouse River, the Daly and the Adelaide River.”

title: Trump shows love for UK’s SNL by sharing skit mocking PM Keir Starmer
description: ‘Triggered with Samara Gill’ host Samara Gill reacts to the UK’s edition of Saturday Night Live.   “In this situation, I think that Trump has basically said, ‘Yeah, this is basically what our conversations look like’,” Ms Gill told Sky News host Andrew Bolt.   “It’s sort of revolutionary to have something on TV that mocks Keir Starmer; it was quite refreshing to watch.”

title: ‘Baffling’: Poll shows 36 per cent of Victorians consider interstate move due to Allan govt
description: Sky News host Danica De Giorgio comments on new polling which confirms 36 per cent of Victorians are considering moving interstate because of the Allan government.

title: ‘Savage barbarity’: Iranian regime plans to inflict serious pain to enemy nations
description: The Australian’s Foreign Editor Greg Sheridan says Iran has shown “savage barbarity” in the war so far.  Mr Sheridan told Sky News host Andrew Bolt that the Russians are the “great winners” out of Iran’s war with the US and Israel.  “Their oil is no longer sanctioned, the price of oil has gone up, there’s less weapons to give to Ukraine, and the Americans have completely taken their eye off Ukraine.”

title: ‘Terrible policies’: Kathy Hochul must make it ‘less painful’ for New York businesses
description: Newsweek Senior Editor-at-Large Josh Hammer says the New York Governor Kathy Hochul must make it “less painful” to do business in the state.  Mr Hammer told Sky News host Rita Panahi that Kathy Hochul has “terrible policies”, which are ruining the state.  “Now there’s the additional layer of just the municipal policies in Zohran Mamdani’s New York City.”

title: Iranian regime will ‘wreak a humanitarian catastrophe’ to win the war
description: The Australian’s Foreign Editor Greg Sheridan says the Iranian regime will “wreak a humanitarian catastrophe” on the Gulf if it can.  Mr Sheridan told Sky News host Andrew Bolt that he wants the US and Israel to “win this war”.  “That is in the interest of everybody.”

title: One Nation revolution labelled ‘real’ as South Australian election results shock the country
description: Sky News host Andrew Bolt details how the One Nation revolution is “real” and coming for the Liberal Party of Australia in both state and federal elections.   “We've seen last week that Pauline Hanson's One Nation revolution is real,” Mr Bolt said.  “The result in South Australia's election – One Nation getting more votes than the Liberals – should finally end the fantasy among some Liberals, especially that One Nation's support in the polls wouldn't be repeated in an actual election.”
